Head of Enterprise Strategy, Jentic

June 4, 2026

Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(2) Erik Wilde

  • Head of Enterprise Strategy at Jentic [https://jentic.com/]
  • Ambassador for the OpenAPI Initiative (OAI) [https://www.openapis.org/]
  • linkedin.com/in/erikwilde [https://www.linkedin.com/in/erikwilde]
  • youtube.com/ErikWilde [https://www.youtube.com/ErikWilde]


APIs and AI

Outline (APIs and AI)

  1. APIs and AI [4]
  2. MCP to the Rescue? [6]
  3. Evolving Your APIs for Agents [3]
  4. Making Workflows Repeatable [2]
  5. With Great Power… [3]
  6. Conclusions [3]
APIs and AI Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(4) Quick Show of Hands

  1. Who used an agentic coding tool this week? (Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, Codex…)
  2. Who has shipped code that takes action based on LLM output in production?
  3. Who has business agents calling APIs autonomously right now?
  • If you raised your hand for #1 you've already met one of the two new API consumers


APIs and AI Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(5) AI Needs Context & Actions

  • LLMs alone: stuck in static training data
  • Need context (private & current data) and actions (do something)
  • Both flow through APIs
  • When this layer is missing → GenAI divide


APIs and AI Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(6) Two New API Consumers

  • Coding agents
    • Help developers build integrations faster
    • Consume your APIs at design time
    • AI inference is discovering and coding with APIs
    • The result is traditional code that behaves deterministically
  • Business agents
    • Call your APIs autonomously to get things done
    • Read your APIs at runtime
    • AI inference is discovering and using APIs
    • The result are agents that behave stochastically
  • Both need things pre-AI developers never required


APIs and AI Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(7) What Changes for You: DX → AX

  • API design: readable for humans (DX) → consumable for machines (AX)
  • API abstraction: endpoints → workflows
  • Credentials: brokered, so agents connect without direct keys
  • Observe & experiment: sandboxes you can learn from
  • If your APIs are already in good shape, you're closer than you think


MCP to the Rescue?

Outline (MCP to the Rescue?)

  1. APIs and AI [4]
  2. MCP to the Rescue? [6]
  3. Evolving Your APIs for Agents [3]
  4. Making Workflows Repeatable [2]
  5. With Great Power… [3]
  6. Conclusions [3]
MCP to the Rescue? Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(9) Just Use MCP?

  • The pitch: APIs are old, MCP is the future
  • Wrap anything in MCP → call it AI-ready
  • Skip the hard API work → straight to AI
  • Just start fresh after 10 years of API landscape work?


MCP to the Rescue? Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(10) Problem 1: Directly Exposing APIs

  • Auto-generate MCP from OpenAPI for every API
  • Context window explosion on real workloads
  • Bad descriptions → bad MCP
  • AI still has to invent workflows across endpoints
  • Now your agents get to have a poor integration experience


MCP to the Rescue? Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(11) Problem 2: Investing in the MCP Layer

  • Hand-craft MCP servers, design the best tools
  • The investment goes into the MCP layer
  • The capability layer underneath remains untouched
  • Better capabilities benefit everyone, not just MCP
  • Everybody's integration experience should benefit


MCP to the Rescue? Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(12) MCP-Washing

  • Skip the API work → jump to MCP → call it AI-ready
  • This is the wrong place to invest from a strategy perspective
  • MCP should be a thin layer, not a fat one
  • Invest in your APIs and then expose them through MCP


MCP to the Rescue? Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(13) Are Your APIs AI-Ready?

  • Invest in your APIs
  • Start by scoring what you have
  • jentic.com/scorecard [https://jentic.com/scorecard]
    • Bad score → fix the basics first
    • Good score → continue


MCP to the Rescue? Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(14) Six Dimensions of AI-Readiness



Evolving Your APIs for Agents

Outline (Evolving Your APIs for Agents)

  1. APIs and AI [4]
  2. MCP to the Rescue? [6]
  3. Evolving Your APIs for Agents [3]
  4. Making Workflows Repeatable [2]
  5. With Great Power… [3]
  6. Conclusions [3]
Evolving Your APIs for Agents Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(16) When LLMs Read Your APIs

  • APIs were typically consumed by developers
  • Developers write code that often fills DX gaps
  • LLMs have more limited context than developers
    • Coding agents shouldn't depend on developers selecting APIs
    • Business agents take the developer out of the picture


Evolving Your APIs for Agents Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(17) Do Your DX Homework

  • Most API descriptions need quality improvements anyway
  • Better descriptions benefit everyone, not just AI agents
    • Complete and meaningful operation summaries
    • Comprehensive examples for all operations
    • Clear error handling
    • Consistent terminology and naming
  • Good DX is the foundation for good AX


Evolving Your APIs for Agents Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(18) Now There's AX Homework, Too

  • AI agents need information developers don't typically require:
    • Everything that developers usually just know
    • Treat every API like an external one for developers with no context
  • OpenAPI Initiative (OAI) might add AX-specific fields to OpenAPI 3.3
  • Until then, leverage description fields strategically


Making Workflows Repeatable

Outline (Making Workflows Repeatable)

  1. APIs and AI [4]
  2. MCP to the Rescue? [6]
  3. Evolving Your APIs for Agents [3]
  4. Making Workflows Repeatable [2]
  5. With Great Power… [3]
  6. Conclusions [3]
Making Workflows Repeatable Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(20) AI Minimalism

  • Use AI when you have to, not when you can
  • AI execution is orders of magnitude more expensive than running code
  • Many business processes are well-defined and repeatable
  • Some business processes may need to be auditable and/or explainable
  • Your AI strategy should be to use as little AI as possible


Making Workflows Repeatable Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(21) What Is Arazzo?

  • Declarative: define the data flow and success criteria
  • OpenAPI Initiative (OAI) [https://www.openapis.org/] standard for API workflows
  • References operationIds from OpenAPI/AsyncAPI documents
  • Discoverable: find and run a workflow without knowing its internals
workflows:
  - workflowId: weekend-getaway
    steps:
      - stepId: listHotels
        description: Retrieve hotels available in the destination city
        operationPath: '{$sourceDescriptions.liteapi-hotel-data.url}#/paths/~1data~1hotels/get'
        parameters:
          - name: cityName
            in: query
            value: $inputs.cityName
        successCriteria:
          - condition: $statusCode == 200
        outputs:
          hotelIds: $response.body#/data[*]/hotelId
  • arazzo.yaml

See the full workflow: arazzo-ui.jentic.com [https://arazzo-ui.jentic.com/?document=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Ffrankkilcommins%2Fagentic-tools-webinar%2Frefs%2Fheads%2Fmain%2Farazzo%2Fweekend-getaway.arazzo.yaml]



With Great Power…

Outline (With Great Power…)

  1. APIs and AI [4]
  2. MCP to the Rescue? [6]
  3. Evolving Your APIs for Agents [3]
  4. Making Workflows Repeatable [2]
  5. With Great Power… [3]
  6. Conclusions [3]
With Great Power… Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(23) Today: Agents Get Keys Directly

  • Agents are less predictable than traditional developers
  • Coding agents produce code → reviewable before it runs
  • Business agents produce actions → live as they're decided
  • Both need limits, but the controls in place are mostly symbolic


With Great Power… Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(24) Secure Centralized Access for Agents

  • Agents should have their own security regime
  • Avoid handing secrets and tokens directly to agents
  • Prevent agents from impersonating users or apps
  • Ensure proper token delegation and downscoping
  • Route all traffic through a centralized access point


With Great Power… Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(25) Sandboxes for Secure Experimentation

  • Create a controlled environment for agents
  • Make capabilities available in the sandbox
  • Prevent agents from accessing production secrets
  • Provide realistic data for testing
  • Reduce risk and encourage safe experimentation


Conclusions

Outline (Conclusions)

  1. APIs and AI [4]
  2. MCP to the Rescue? [6]
  3. Evolving Your APIs for Agents [3]
  4. Making Workflows Repeatable [2]
  5. With Great Power… [3]
  6. Conclusions [3]
Conclusions Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(27) Three Levels of API Improvement

  1. Improve descriptions — change API description: easy, immediate results
  2. Improve definitions — change the API itself: harder, improves capabilities
  3. Start platform engineering — built once, used across many APIs:
    • Workflow orchestration (open standard: Arazzo)
    • Credential brokering for safe agent access
    • Sandboxes for safe experimentation
  • If your APIs are already in good shape, you're closer than you think


Conclusions Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents

(28) Start Tomorrow: Score Your APIs

jentic.com/scorecard [https://jentic.com/scorecard]



Conclusions Erik Wilde: APIs for the Age of Agents
